dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've been up all night analyzing what you brought back, and I think I finally understand.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} aren't ruins at all: they're a monitoring system. Ancient builders created a network to track seismic activity, water tables, and mineral deposits. The 'lights' are warning indicators, and the {quest_item} patterns are data readouts. The {enemy_type} were responding to vibrations. You managed to {quest_action}, and {quest_stakes} turned out to be engineering so advanced it looks like magic. {target_npc} is already adapting the knowledge.
dialogueChunks.outro: We didn't just solve a mystery: we recovered technology that could protect the settlement for generations. Take this, with the deepest gratitude.

playerResponses.text: Ancient engineering mistaken for magic. That's the best kind of answer.
Technology that predicts disasters? That knowledge is worth more than gold.

dialogueChunks.intro: The spirals stopped, {player_name}. The crops are growing normally again, and the {enemy_type} have returned to their usual patterns.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} and I believe the phenomenon was cyclical: a natural process occurring once every few decades when underground minerals shift. The {quest_item} deposits were crystallized minerals pushed to the surface by the same pressure. You managed to {quest_action}, and {quest_stakes} are answered: the earth breathing, in its own slow way.
dialogueChunks.outro: You turned a mystery that terrified us into knowledge that empowers us. Here, a proper reward for a proper scholar's work.

playerResponses.text: The earth breathing. I like that. A mystery with a poetic answer.
Glad the mystery had a natural explanation. Not everything needs to be sinister.

dialogueChunks.intro: I need to sit down for this, {player_name}. The implications of what we've discovered are staggering.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} wasn't just sealed: it was deliberately hidden. {target_npc} translated enough of the writing to understand: the device described in the instructions is a weapon, built during an ancient war and locked away when peace was achieved. You managed to {quest_action}, and the answer to {quest_stakes} was a warning. We've resealed the cave and destroyed the instructions.
dialogueChunks.outro: Some mysteries are solved and celebrated. Others are solved and buried. This is the second kind. Take this, and carry the knowledge quietly.

playerResponses.text: A weapon sealed by people wise enough to lock it away. We should honor that wisdom.
Destroyed the instructions? Good. Some answers are better left forgotten.

dialogueChunks.intro: The silence is gone, {player_name}. The birds are back, the insects are humming, and {quest_focus} is quiet.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: What woke up in that tower was simpler than we feared: a hibernating colony of bioluminescent creatures that produce energy fields disrupting animal senses. The {quest_item} ring was mineral buildup from their excretions. The {enemy_type} fled because the fields caused them pain. You managed to {quest_action}, and {target_npc} collected specimens for study. The colony has returned to dormancy.
dialogueChunks.outro: Not a curse, not a weapon: just creatures we didn't know existed. Take this, with thanks for having the courage to find the mundane truth behind the extraordinary.

playerResponses.text: Bioluminescent creatures and mineral excretions. Reality is stranger than any legend.
The natural world never stops surprising. Glad the mystery is solved.
